signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: Middle East escalation drives safe-haven demand — JPY COT LF net short −65,309 (crowded carry). USD/JPY at 159.15; 160.00 remains the key level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings; intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 160. Watch for a move above 160.00 to confirm increased intervention risk.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7163 — CFTC LF net long +41,675 (week Apr 14), long%=72.0%. HV30 at 6.5%, ATM IV (FXE chain) at 8.2% — options pricing a 1.7 vol-pt premium over realized. Retail sentiment 14% long / 86% short — extreme short bias. Watch for LF delta turning positive or volatility compression to shift the read.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Positioning + Macro
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1769 — EUR LF net short −72, long%=50.0%. German ZEW sentiment −17.2 (worst since Dec 2023) amid Iran war concerns. ECB's Guindos: central bank sees private credit as key risk to financial stability. Watch for a break below 1.1700 to confirm a downside move.

signals.title: GBP/USD — CB + Technical
signals.text: BoE 4.50% vs Fed 3.75% — 75bp GBP carry premium supports the pair structurally. GBP LF net long +28,426, AM constructive — positioning aligned with the carry read. GBP/USD at 1.3520; 1.3600 is the next resistance on a continuation. Watch for a break above 1.3600 to confirm further upside.

signals.title: NZD/USD — Retail Sentiment
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5910 — retail sentiment 33% long / 67% short. No extreme positioning or significant COT data to flag. NZD/USD remains in a range; watch for a break of 0.5900 or 0.6000 for direction.
